reason_not_scored: This is an academic conference presentation/research methodology video about defining a VR curriculum for laparoscopic colorectal surgery. It contains slides with titles, research methods, Delphi consensus results, and expert benchmark data. There is no actual FLS task performance video (peg transfer, pattern cut, endoloop, or suture tying). The content is educational/instructional with narration and research findings, not a trainee or expert performing an FLS task.
content_summary: Research presentation by V. Palter, M. Graafland, M. Schijven, T. Grantcharov on defining proficiency-based VR curriculum for laparoscopic colorectal surgery. Includes Delphi consensus methodology with 19 international experts, task relevance survey, expert benchmark levels for 8 core tasks (coordination, grasping, cutting, clip applying, lifting/grasping, handling intestines, fine dissection, stitch and square knot), and conclusions on proficiency-based training standards.

teaching_content.presentation_focus: Research methodology and expert consensus for VR curriculum development in laparoscopic surgery

cannot_score: No actual FLS task performance is present in these frames. This is a research presentation slide deck, not video of a trainee performing peg transfer, pattern cut, endoloop, or suture tying.
